title: Even Bad Men Need Company in the Night
description:
“Young Flower” by Samuel Jensen Pathetic, really, how late in life he had come into his beauty. It didn’t even have the decency to be the normal kind of beauty—no, his was the beauty of a svelte but surprisingly strong former prisoner of a gulag, rangy and bearded, quiet about the before parts of his […]

The post Even Bad Men Need Company in the Night appeared first on
hook: A story about a man whose beauty arrives too late, carrying the weight of a gulag survivor's quiet strength—late-bloomer masculinity rendered strange and luminous.

title: Harriet Clark's novel 'The Hill' draws on her 37 years visiting her mom in prison
description: Clark was a baby in 1981 when her mother, a member of a radical left-wing group, acted as a getaway driver in the robbery of a Brink's truck. Her new novel is based on the years that followed.
hook: A novelist spent 37 years visiting her mother in prison after a radical heist gone wrong. Her debut novel is the daughter's reckoning with that impossible intimacy.

title: No Picnic Feasts at Film Forum
description: In 1985, downtown cinephile Phil Hartman used profits from the Great Jones Cafe, a beloved Cajun-inspired NoHo bar he cofounded two years earlier, to direct his first—and, to date, only completed—feature film, No Picnic. A noirish shaggy-dog punk comedy photographed by experimental filmmaker Peter Hutton on lustrous black-and
hook: How a downtown cinephile built a Cajun bar, then turned a film forum into a temple for cinema—a love letter to obsolete cultural institutions and their architects.

title: When the Earth Gives Way
description: A town ninety miles north of the Arctic Circle does not need two city halls, two high schools, and two fire stations. To establish a new city, the old one must die, and this project to destroy and rebuild central Kiruna—known as the “urban” or “social transformation”—has now been ongoing for more than two decades.
hook: A Swedish city north of the Arctic Circle is being systematically erased and rebuilt elsewhere. What does it mean to destroy a place so bureaucratically, so completely?

title: A Beastie Boy Returns to the Mic, With Some Help
description: "Thanks to an assist from his sons, Mike D is set to release “Thank You,” the first new music from a member of the pioneering rap trio in 15 years."
hook: Fifteen years after the Beastie Boys fractured, Mike D returns to rap with his sons as collaborators—a late-career reinvention built on inherited legacy and family repair.
